# The Valuation Node
> Independent research and learning on Indian markets, authored by Gajji Srinath.

The Valuation Node publishes original equity valuation and credit analysis of Indian companies,
alongside a structured free learning library covering accounting, corporate finance, valuation,
credit analysis, ESG, fintech, and markets. All content cites sources and shows its methodology.
No investment advice is given. No paid promotions.

## Research
Original analysis of Indian companies, sectors, and credit instruments.
URL: https://valuationnode.com/research

## Learning Library
Free structured curriculum for Indian market analysis:
- Accounting (income statement, balance sheet, cash flows, ratios)
- Corporate Finance (WACC, capital structure, DCF)
- Valuation (DCF, relative valuation, sector-specific multiples)
- Credit Analysis (CRISIL/ICRA rating frameworks, Altman Z-score, bond pricing)
- Markets & Instruments (equities, bonds, derivatives, REITs, ETFs)
- ESG & Sustainable Finance (ESG scores, green bonds, carbon accounting)
- Fintech & Digital Finance (payments, digital lending, account aggregators)
URL: https://valuationnode.com/learn

## Tools
13 free financial calculators for Indian investors: SIP, step-up SIP, goal SIP, DCF sensitivity,
WACC, CAGR, future and present value, compound interest, rule of 72, EMI, loan prepayment,
and inflation-adjusted returns.
URL: https://valuationnode.com/tools

## Glossary
Searchable glossary of 170+ Indian market and finance terms, each with a definition, formula
where applicable, why it matters, and an example.
URL: https://valuationnode.com/learn/glossary

## Learn-by-Doing
Five interactive modules: build a DCF step by step, read an income statement line by line,
compute ratios from raw statements, compare two companies, and spot financial red flags.
URL: https://valuationnode.com/learn/by-doing

## Markets
Nifty 50 company reference data and SEBI compliance calendar.
URL: https://valuationnode.com/markets

## Author
Gajji Srinath, Founder and Editor, MBA Candidate at NIT Rourkela.
Contact: srinath@valuationnode.com
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gajji-srinath/
URL: https://valuationnode.com/about/author

## Editorial Principles
1. All sources are cited.
2. Methodology is shown (models, assumptions, data sources).
3. Uncertainty is named, not hidden.
4. No investment advice.
5. No sponsored content.

## Sitemap
https://valuationnode.com/sitemap.xml
